---
title: 'Practice Problems: Function Basics'
author: ""
output: 
  github_document:
    toc: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# This chunk sets up the environment.
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.align="center")

library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)

# Common plotting theme for consistency
common_theme <- theme_minimal() + 
                theme(axis.title = element_text(face="bold"),
                      plot.title = element_text(face="bold", hjust = 0.5))

```

1. Consider the function defined by:
$$
f(x) = \begin{cases} 
\frac{1}{x}, & 2 < x < 5 \\
0, & \text{otherwise}.
\end{cases}
$$
   (i) What is the **Domain** of this function?
   (ii) What is the **Support** of this function?

2. Find the inverse function $x = f^{-1}(y)$ for the function:
$$f(x) = 3x - 7$$

3. Explain why the function $f(x) = x^2 - 4$ does not have an inverse on the domain $\mathbb{R}$.

4. Find the domain of the function:
$$f(x) = \log(5 - x)$$

5. Simplify the expression:
$$f(x) = e^{3 \log(x)}$$

6. Plot out the following piecewise function and rewrite it using the Indicator Function notation $I_A(x)$:
$$
f(x) = \begin{cases} 
10, & -2 \le x \le 2 \\
0, & \text{otherwise}.
\end{cases}
$$

7. Find the inverse function $x = f^{-1}(y)$ for the function:
$$f(x) = \frac{x}{x + 1}$$

8. Find the inverse function $x = f^{-1}(y)$ for the function:
$$f(x) = \log(x - 3)$$

9. Consider the function:
$$
f(x) = \begin{cases} 
\sqrt{1 - x^2}, & -1 < x < 1 \\
0, & \text{otherwise}.
\end{cases}
$$
   (i) Determine the domain of $f$.
   (ii) Determine the support of $f$.
